name.text: Calculating the area of a given 8 sided figure

questiontext.text:
A regular octagon has center O. If the distance from the center to a vertex is OA = {#r#} cm, find the total area of the octagon.

Give your answer correct to 2 decimal places.

[[jsxgraph width="400px" height="400px"]] var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, { boundingbox: [-10, 10, 10, -10], axis: false, showNavigation: false, showCopyright: false }); var n = 8; var R = 7; // Visual radius /* Create Vertices */ var points = []; for (var i = 0; i < n; i++) { var angle = (2 * Math.PI * i / n); points.push(board.create('point', [R * Math.cos(angle), R * Math.sin(angle)], {visible: false})); } /* Create Octagon */ board.create('polygon', points, { fillColor: '#f0f0f0', borders: {strokeWidth: 2, strokeColor: 'black'} }); var O = board.create('point', [0, 0], {name: 'O', color: 'black', size: 3}); var A = points[0]; var B = points[1]; /* Show the triangle slice from the example */ board.create('segment', [O, A], {dash: 2, strokeColor: 'gray'}); board.create('segment', [O, B], {dash: 2, strokeColor: 'gray'}); /* Labels */ board.create('text', [3.5, 0.5, "{#r#} cm"], {fontSize: 14}); board.create('text', [2.5, 3.5, "{#r#} cm"], {fontSize: 14}); /* Angle Arc */ board.create('angle', [A, O, B], {radius: 1.5, name: "45°"}); [[/jsxgraph]]

Area = [[input:ans1]] cm2 [[validation:ans1]][[feedback:prt1]]

generalfeedback.text:
Worked Solution

A regular octagon is composed of 8 congruent isosceles triangles meeting at the centre O.

Step 1: Identify the properties of one triangle (\(\triangle AOB\))

  • The two equal sides (radii) are \(r = {@r@}\text{ cm}\).
  • The central angle is calculated as \(360^\circ \div 8 = 45^\circ\).

Step 2: Calculate the area of one triangle

Using the Sine Rule for Area, \(\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2}ab\sin C\):

\[ \text{Area of } \triangle AOB = \frac{1}{2} \times {@r@} \times {@r@} \times \sin(45^\circ) \] \[ \text{Area of } \triangle AOB = \frac{1}{2} \times {@r^2@} \times 0.7071 \] \[ \text{Area of } \triangle AOB \approx {@round(area_tri*10000)/10000@}\text{ cm}^2 \]

Step 3: Calculate the total area of the octagon

Since there are 8 such triangles in the octagon:

\[ \text{Total Area} = 8 \times \text{Area of } \triangle AOB \] \[ \text{Total Area} = 8 \times {@round(area_tri*10000)/10000@} \] \[ \text{Total Area} = {@ta@}\text{ cm}^2 \]

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.
